english: I. the space within a thing, 1. the interior, the inside, pûg-pai naṅ kun (phug pa'i nang kun) the whole interior of the cavern Mil.; rdziṅ-pai, doṅ-gi naṅ (rdzing pa'i, dong gi nang) the interior of a basin, of a pit (e.g. being filled up) Dzl.; ḱaṅ-pai naṅ pyag-dár byéd-pa (khang pa'i nang phyag dar byed pa) to sweep the inside of a house Dzl. - 2. space, room, apartment, chamber col. - 3. dwelling, domicile, house, esp. C. - 4. meton inmates, family, household, *naṅ tśaṅ* (nang tshang) W. the whole family. - 5. the interior (spiritually), heart, mind, soul, ye-śés naṅ-na śar (ye shes nang na shar) wisdom begins to shine in the mind; żen-dzin naṅ-nas grol (zhen 'dzin nang nas grol) affection, interest, disappears from the heart Glr. - 6. sometimes adv. for naṅ-na (nang na). II. naṅ-gi (nang gi), genit., used 1. as an adj.: inner, inward, esoteric (opp. to pyi (phyi)), naṅ-gi khrims (nang gi khrims), naṅ-krims (nang khrims), a private law, an esoteric precept or doctrine not intended for the public; *ge-dúṅ-gyi naṅ-tím dhaṅ .gal tśe* (dge 'dun gyi nang khrims dang 'gal tshe) C. if priests violate their special moral duties, (very different from naṅ-pai khrims (nang pa'i khrims) the Buddhist law, merely opp. to Brahmanism); naṅ-gi sbyin-pa (nang gi sbyin pa) inward offerings, i.e. spiritual sacrifices, opp. to outward and material offerings; but Dzl. ༢༣༤, 4 it denotes personal sacrifices, the surrendering of parts of our own self, e.g. a member of the body, opp. to outward property; the meaning also reminds of Rom. 12, 1, and I Pet 2, 5. - naṅ-gi byá-ba (nang gi bya ba) internal affairs Glr.; v. also the compounds. - 2. for naṅ-na (nang na) among, amidst, frq. c. accus.: bu naṅ-gi ťa čuṅ (bu nang gi tha chung), pûg-ron naṅ-gi čuṅ-ṅu (phug ron nang gi chung ngu) Dzl. the smallest among etc.: for dé-dag-gi naṅ-na (de dag gi nang na) of it, of them, among them etc.: naṅ-gi čuṅ-ṅu (nang gi chung ngu) the least of them Dzl.; naṅ-gi lhá-mo sṅá-ma (nang gi lha mo snga ma) the foremost among the goddesses; sometimes more pleon., without distinct reference to a preceding noun, Dzl. ༧༨, 18; ༧༣༤, 16 (where Sch. prob. translates incorr.). III. with la (la), na (na), du (du), nas (nas); 1. as sbst., acc. to the significations given above, e.g. nad-pai naṅ-du ǰúg-pa (nad pa'i nang du 'jug pa) to go into the room of a sick person Wdn.; dei naṅ-du ydan-drás-te (de'i nang du gdan drangs te) inviting into their house Mil. -- 2. as adv. naṅ-na (nang na) in it, therein, within, among it or them; naṅ-du (nang du) and naṅ-la (nang la) thereinto, into it; naṅ-nas (nang nas) out, thereout, from among; among it or them = naṅ-na (nang na). -- 3. postp : in, into, among etc., e.g. rdziṅ-gi naṅ-na ḱrus byéd-pa (rdzing gi nang na khrus byed pa) Dzl. to bathe in a pond, čui naṅ-du żúgs-pa (chu'i nang du zhugs pa) to go into the water; groṅ-kyér dei naṅ daṅ pyi-rol-na (grong khyer de'i nang dang phyi rol na) in the town and out of it Dzl.; *sém-mi naṅ-na zér-pa* (sems mi nang na zer pa) W. he said to himself; snai naṅ-nas byuṅ (sna'i nang nas byung) it came out of his nose (again) Dzl.; mii naṅ-na(s) bzaṅ-pa żig (mi'i nang na(s) bzang pa zhig) one very beautiful among men Dzl.; gliṅ dé-rnams-kyi naṅ-na(s) mčóg-tu gyúr-pa (gling de rnams kyi nang na(s) mchog tu gyur pa) the most important among or of these countries Glr. (here at least the sing. is as frq. als the plur.); in col. language the word is much used, though often inaccurately; so it is frq. employed, where the later literature has naṅ-la (nang la), naṅ-nas (nang nas); *wáṅ-gi naṅ-na* (dbang gi nang na) by force; *só-me naṅ-na zer gos* (so me nang na zer dgos) W. that should have been mentioned, when it was fresh (in remembrance); *lo toṅ-ni naṅ-na tsá-pig ma tsar* (lo stong ni nang na tsha phig ma tshar) not yet quite in a thousand years, i.e. it is not full a thousand years W. - There is still to be noticed: naṅ (nang) = naṅ-mo (nang mo). -- naṅ-méd-la (nang med la) col. frq. suddenly; in B. of rare occurrence; naṅ-méd nor rnyéd-pa (nang med nor rnyed pa) to become rich unexpectedly S.g. Comp. and deriv. naṅ-kyóg (nang khyog) Sch.: having legs bending inward, bandy-legged. -- naṅ-skór (nang skor) v. skór-ba (skor ba) extr. -- naṅ-khrims (nang khrims) v. above. -- naṅ-król (nang khrol), vulgo -rol (-rol), bowels, entrails, intestines; also any separate part of them; naṅ-król dróṅ-ba (nang khrol drong ba) spasmodic contractions of the bowels Sch.; naṅ-krol-bżág (nang khrol bzhag) seems in Lexx. to be taken synon. with mnyam-bżag (mnyam bzhag). -- naṅ-góg (nang gog) v. ter (gter). -- *naṅ-gyóg* (nang rgyag) W. a large bolt, door-bar. -- naṅ-ča (nang cha) = naṅ-krol (nang khrol). -- naṅ-čags-su (nang chags su) in one's self, in one's own mind Sch. -- naṅ-rǰe (nang rje) minister of the interior, home-minister Sch. -- naṅ-lta (nang lta) Glr 89, 11? -- naṅ-ťáb byéd-pa (nang 'thab byed pa) to be involved in intestine war Pth., = naṅ-krúgs (nang 'khrugs). -- naṅ-dág (nang dag) 1. Sch. 'the interior being cleansed'. 2. col. (or naṅ-brtags (nang brtags)?) v. snaṅ (snang). -- naṅ-don (nang don) the intrinsic meaning, the true sense, naṅ-don rtóg-pa (nang don rtog pa) to investigate, to study, the real meaning; *naṅ-don tóg-ḱen* (nang don rtog mkhan),
